title: daucus gingidium subsp. majoricus
intro: Daucus gingidium subsp. majoricus is a subspecies of the plant known as giant carrot, a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that grows in the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Daucus gingidium subsp. majoricus include its potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its possible effects on digestive and cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which are commonly used in folk medicine to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
